attacking_style: Launches repeated late surges in breakaways within final 3km, targeting summit finishes and KOM points rather than stage wins. Attacks to disintegrate groups and position for secondary objectives.
team_tactics: Serves dual role: primary leadout man for team sprinters (MILANO) in flat stages while pursuing personal breakaway opportunities when released. Team surrenders GC positioning to allow breakaway freedom.
terrain_preference: Climbing stages where breakaways contest KOM points and summit finishes. Performs strongly in time trials (48 seconds ahead of quality specialists). Less effective on pure flat sprint stages.
vulnerability: Tactical inexperience in small group sprint dynamics—launches finishing efforts too early and fades. Injury-prone (elbow issues recurring). Lacks superior finishing speed when caught by faster finishers.
key_patterns: Repeatedly attacks final 3km in breakaways to fracture groups, not secure wins (2019 x2)
Targets KOM points and intermediate objectives over stage victories across multiple Vueltas (2018, 2019)
Acts as leadout domestique for team sprinters when not in breakaways (2023 Vuelta)
Shows strong time trial capability exceeding known specialists by significant margins (2020)
Demonstrates willingness to assume virtual GC lead within breakaways for team tactical control (2017)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 crash on previously injured elbow suggests recurring injury concerns compromising leadout duties; no recent breakaway success noted
